subject: Re: [opensuse] Unstable system - who is the culprit?
body: On Thu, 2008-02-07 at 10:13 +0100, Per Jessen wrote: > Randall R Schulz wrote: > > > Offhand, I don't know how the efficiency of today's power supplies > > varies with the percentage of their rated output they're producing. In > > other words, does a higher-capacity PSU consume more mains power for a > > given level of consumption on its output side? If so, you wouldn't > > want to have such a large PSU, since that would be wasteful. > > I haven't looked at switchmode technology for quite some time, but I > would expect a fairly fixed efficiency in the 80-90% range, quite > probably more. There will be a minimum consumption required in order > to achieve optimal operation, but otherwise I think the output power > will be e.g. 80% of the input power, and the rest is turned into heat. or to say it another way...(at 80% efficiency) a 150 watt supply or a 500 watt supply should either one pull the same current from the mains, if they alternately supply the same 80 watt load (ie MB)...approximately 100 watts from the mains in each example. It would NOT be wasteful to the environment...(but maybe to your wallet). Tom in NM
model_reason: Legitimate email conversation about power supplies

body: Ukraine shoots down all 18 missiles aimed at Kyiv. Watch this special moment one graduate shared with her immigrant parents. Now, here we go with Tuesday's news. Russia launches 'exceptional' air attack in Kyiv Loud explosions boomed over Kyiv as Russia attacked early Tuesday in an apparent attempt to overwhelm Ukraineâs air defenses, but Ukraine's forces thwarted the intense raid, shooting down all 18 missiles aimed at the capital. Russia's attack was âexceptional in its density" the head of the Kyiv military administration said. â¢This is the eighth time this month that Russian air raids have targeted the capital, a clear escalation after weeks of lull and ahead of a much-anticipated Ukrainian counter-offensive. It also came as President Volodymyr Zelenksyy concluded a whirlwind European tour to greet Ukraineâs key wartime allies. â¢The attack came as leaders from across Europe are heading to Iceland Tuesday for a rare summit of the 46-nation Council of Europe that will once more step up support for member state Ukraine. â¢More on Russia: A Justice Department special counsel sharply criticized the department and FBI for the investigation of Russian interference in the 2016 election. The shooting was "honestly one of the most horrific and difficult days that Farmington has ever had as a community," Hebbe said. subject: Problem with ClickUp Dashboard Metric Updates
body:
Dear Customer Support,

I am reaching out to inform you about a problem I am experiencing with my ClickUp dashboard. The campaign metrics are failing to update, and I suspect there might be an issue with the integration between ClickUp and PDFfiller. I have already attempted to restart ClickUp and review the API connections, but the problem persists.

Here is a brief description of the issue:

Event: Campaign metrics are not updating on the ClickUp dashboard.
Cause: Potential integration issue with PDFfiller.
Attempted Solutions: Restarted ClickUp, verified API connections, but the issue remains unresolved.

I would be grateful if you could look into this matter and provide a resolution at your earliest convenience. Please inform me if you require any additional details from me to address this issue.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

subject: Hi! So Nice To Meet You!
body: Hi! So Nice To Meet You! Hello new friend! We are Elsie & Emma and it's so nice to meet you. Welcome to the A Beautiful Mess newsletter! We are sisters and we've been blogging for over 15 years. Our site is a place for creative people, like you! We share a huge mi Hello new friend! We are Elsie & Emma and it's so nice to meet you. Welcome to the A Beautiful Mess newsletter! We are sisters and we've been blogging for over 15 years. Our site is a place for creative people, like you! We share a huge mix of easy recipes, crafts, and home decorating ideas. We grew up with an art teacher mother who taught us that staying home and making stuff was the path to a happy life, and we've never looked back. (Although we have been known to leave our houses on occasion.) We hope to inspire you to make stuffânothing would make us happier! Since we're just getting to know you, here is a mix of some of our most popular blog posts in different categories you might enjoy: Do you like crafting? Probably our most made and most copied craft post of all time are these marbled clay ring dishes that we posted back in 2014. Second most popular tutorial? How to make your own bath bombs. You can see our entire craft archive here. We have something for everyone, like sewing tutorials, jewelry making, clay projects, scrapbooking, and tons and tons of garlands and wreaths. Lol. Do you love home decor? We have hundreds of DIY projects on our site like this popular built-in bookcase hack. We also share lots of personal ideas and advice like this post about when an open floor plan is too open. We love hearing what our readers think, too! Are you into cooking or baking? We are! Here are a bunch of our best cookie recipes and easy no-bake desserts. And if you need something for dinner tonight, check out these 30-minute meal ideas. We also have a podcast. Our listeners say it's like catching up with an old friend. Here are a few episodes you might want to try if you want to get to know us:Elsie's Life Map and Emma's Life Map. We both grew up conservative evangelicals and feel pretty different about that today. Elsie shares more about this topic here, and you can read Emma's point of view here. But, we mostly share about things like decor trends and goal setting. It's a mix! There are three great ways to stay in touch with us. First, read our blog (which we update daily.) Next, subscribe to our podcast. We have new episodes every Monday. And, you can follow us on Instagram. We'd like to hear about you and things that you're interested in! Please hit reply and tell us three things about yourself, including your current favorite hobby. xo. Elsie & Emma 731 S. Pickwick AvenueSpringfield, MO 65802, United States Donât want these emails anymore? You can Unsubscribe or Manage Preferences.
model_reason: Legitimate newsletter from A Beautiful Mess, no suspicious language or links.

subject: Suggestion for Mercury Language: Enhanced Error Reporting
body:
Dear Aetheros Support Team,

I've been working with Mercury for the past few months on a complex API project, and while I appreciate its efficiency, I believe the error reporting could be improved.

Currently, the error messages can be a bit cryptic, especially when dealing with nested function calls or asynchronous operations. It would be incredibly helpful if the error messages provided more context, such as:

* The specific line number and file where the error occurred.
* A stack trace to pinpoint the sequence of function calls leading to the error.
* More descriptive error messages that clearly explain the root cause.

These improvements would significantly speed up debugging and enhance the overall developer experience.

Thank you for considering my suggestion.

Sincerely,
John Doe

The focus of the conference includes, but is not limited=20 to the areas listed below. * Control Systems: Neural Networks Control, Fuzzy Logic Control, Genetic Algorithms & Evolutionary Control, Model-Predictive Control, Adaptive and Optimal Control, Intelligent Control Systems, Robotics and Automation, Fault Diagnosis, Intelligent agents & Multiagent Systems, Industrial Automations Hybrid Systems; Fuzzy Evolutionary Systems, Fuzzy Expert Systems, Fuzzy Neural Systems, Neural Genetic Systems, Neural-Fuzzy-Genetic Systems, Hybrid Systems for Optimization. * Optimization and Prediction: Multi-criteria Optimization, Signal Processing, Time Series Analysis & Prediction, System Identification, Data Mining & Knowledge Discovery, Intelligent Information Systems, Image Processing, Parallel Computing applications in Identification & Control, Pattern Recognition, Clustering, Classification. * Decision Making: Intelligent Databases & Information Retrieval, Case-Based Reasoning, Decision Analysis, Dynamic Systems Modeling, Decision Support Systems, Qualitative and Approximate-Reasoning Modeling. * Security: Intrusion Detection, Privilege Determining, Denial-of-Service Resistance, Attack Resistance, Cryptographic Mechanisms and Protocols, Mobile Environment Security, Wireless Security, Access control, Database=20 Security, Identification and Authentication. * Workshop: A workshop dedicated to Satellite Control and Communications will take place in parallel to the conference sessions. Topics might come for=20 example from the field of Intelligent Ground Control Systems, Autonomous Satellite Cluster Control, Intelligent Telemetry Processing, and Intelligent Antenna Systems. SUBMISSION OF PAPERS Papers reporting original and unpublished research or application results pertaining to the above and related topics are solicited. Papers will be reviewed on the basis of two pages extended abstracts of sufficient detail to permit reasonable evaluation. Accepted papers will appear in the conference proceedings with an ISBN. The final version of accepted papers should be of six pages long. Submissions should include the title, author(s), affiliation(s), e-mail address(es), tel/fax numbers, postal address(es) and keywords on the first page. Papers should be submitted on the conference web site: http://aista.tudor.lu/aista2004. If web=20 submission is not possible, manuscripts should be sent as an attachment via email to one of the Program Chairs listed below on before the deadline date of September 5, 2004. A special issue of the international of Integrated=20 Computer-Aided Engineering (ICAE) will be devoted to publish extended versions of the=20 best papers=20 presented at AISTA'04. subject: Invitation to Attend the Clinical Trials and Innovation in Asian Oncology Congress 2025, Tokyo
body: Dear Amanda, Greetings. I am delighted to invite you to the Clinical Trials and Innovation in Asian Oncology Congress 2025, co-organized with Euroleukemia, taking place from May 15-16, 2025 in Tokyo, Japan. This premier event will bring together the world's foremost clinicians, researchers, and industry leaders to explore the latest innovations and challenges in oncology and hematologyâfrom bench to bedside. Our meeting will cover critical advancements in the understanding and treatment of solid tumors and hematological malignancies. We will engage in insightful discussions on cutting-edge therapies, including CAR-T and cell therapies, immunotherapy, new oncology drugs, imaging techniques, and the essential role of supportive and palliative care. In addition to a rich scientific program, we are excited to offer a unique opportunity for the global research community. Accepted abstracts will be published in Frontiers journal, providing valuable visibility for your work. We encourage you to submit an abstract in any of the following categories: Cellular and Molecular Biology of Solid Tumors Cellular and Molecular Biology of Hematological Malignancies Imaging in Solid Tumors and Hematological Malignancies New Drugs in Oncology New Drugs in Hematological Oncology CAR-T and Other Cell Therapies for Hematological Malignancies and Solid Tumors Immunotherapy in Solid Tumors and Hematological Malignancies Supportive Care in Oncology and Hematology Palliative Care The top 9 abstracts will be selected for oral presentations, offering the chance to showcase your work in front of a distinguished audience of experts in the field. Join us in shaping the future of oncology and hematology by sharing your insights, learning from others, and forging new collaborations. Please kindly reply to visit our conference website for more information: https://www.ctiaocongress.com/. We look forward to your participation in this exciting event. Warm regards, Ahma Inoue
model_reason: Legitimate invitation to a conference with a clear purpose and no suspicious links or attachments.

subject: Problem encountered with Investment Projections
body: Observation: The investment projections have been imprecise lately. Hypothesis: The data feed might be malfunctioning. Action taken: Restarted the analytics software and reverified the data. I am reaching out to report an issue with the investment projections that have been inaccurate in the recent past. I suspect the data feed could be the root cause and have already restarted the analytics software and reverified the data. Kindly examine this and provide an appropriate resolution at the earliest.
model_reason: Legitimate email with a clear purpose and no suspicious language or links.

subject: Ripple Rewards XRP Holders: Find Out How
body:
View this email in your browser

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

In an unexpected chain of occurrences, Ripple has ultimately resolved its significant judicial conflict with the SEC, marking a substantial victory for both Ripple and the broader digital currency community. This defining success establishes established channels for technological advancement and paves the way toward a more supportive regulatory environment.

To mark this historic moment, Ripple is recognizing dedicated XRP holders with an limited allocation of RLUSD, Rippleâs innovative stablecoin anchored to the dollar. RLUSD, already live on the XRP blockchain, is designed to meet corporate demands, enabling fast, low-cost, and fully compliant worldwide remittances and trading. With RLUSD trading volumes exceeding half a billion dollars in second quarter only, this exclusive distribution highlights Rippleâs unwavering support to its community and innovation. Approved participants can now claim RLUSD equivalent to 17% of XRP assets, though the pool is limited to just 5,000,000 RLUSD tokens.

However, the celebration doesnât end there. Ripple has further surprised the financial sector by finalizing the Hidden Road deal, a global trading intermediary, for $1.25B. This landmark acquisition puts Ripple at the crossroads of crypto markets and legacy financial infrastructure, unlocking unprecedented opportunities for professional investment sector to embrace blockchain-based assets with effortless integration and market liquidity.

In addition to this substantial investment, Rippleâs platform within XRPL continues to accelerate growth with the launch of several worldwide fiat-supported digital currencies, including USDC, XS

subject: NedBank Account Statement - 18-09-26
body: Click here to read more Dear Customer Welcome to the movement towards a cleaner, greener, paperless world. Attached to this email is your encrypted electronic statement with enhanced security. To open it simply follow the step-by-step instructions below. Also, please advise us if your email address changes that way we can ensure that you receive your eStatement safely, on time, every time. By opting for eStatements, youre making a tangible contribution to shrink your, and Nedbanks, carbon footprint. As South Africas green bank, and to show our commitment to a cleaner, greener world, Nedbank will donate 25c to The Green Trust for every eStatement we send. Its the small things we do together that add up to make a big difference in curbing climate change. Opening your encrypted statement To open your statement you will need Adobe Reader version 5 or higher. Click here to download. Step 1: Double-click on the attachment. Step 2: Type in your password. Step 3: Click 'OK'. This message has been digitally signed to enable you to verify both its origin and integrity. If the message has been tampered with in any way, a security warning will alert you when you open the mail. To verify the senders digital identity simply click on the red ribbon. Your Password Your password is your account number. To help you avoid confusion if you have multiple current accounts, simply look at the name of the attachment in the email the last four digits are the same as the last four digits in your account number. However, please be sure that you use your full account number when typing in your password(s). Statement Cycle Nedbank will email your statement in accordance with your chosen statement cycle (eg monthly). If we encounter a problem delivering your eStatement eg mailbox is full we will continue trying for up to 24 hours. Thereafter the email will be deemed undeliverable. If your statements remain undelivered for three consecutive cycles, Nedbank will cease to send you eStatements. To resume the service, simply contact Nedbank on 0860 555 111 and ensure that your eStatements delivery details are up to date. subject: Critical Issue Report: Integration Failure
body:
Dear Customer Support,

I am reporting a significant problem that has caused delays in our project. Several integrations failed simultaneously, and we have not been able to resolve the issue despite our efforts. The problem began after a recent update, suggesting possible conflicts or API changes. We have restarted services, checked logs, and verified settings, but the issue persists.

We would appreciate your urgent assistance in investigating this matter and providing a solution or guidance on how to resolve it. Please let us know if there are additional steps we can take or if you need further information from us.

Thank you for your attention.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
model_reason: Legitimate email with a clear and specific issue report, no suspicious language or links.
